chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Crl. Bail Appln. No.D-59 of 2025. DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE. 1. For orders on M.A. No.463/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on O/objection at flag-A. 3. For orders on M.A. No.464/2025 (Ex/A) 4. For orders on M.A. No.465/2025 5. For hearing of protective bail. 16.07.2025 Mr. Saifulah Soomro, Advocate alongwith applicants. ******** 1. Urgency Granted. 2. Deferred. 3. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 4&5. Through instant bail application, applicants Muhammad Amin Khan and Haji Ghulam Mustafa both sons of Haji Muhammad Sharif seek protective bail in Crime No.58/2025 registered at Police Station, “Khambhra” under Sections 365-A, 302, 324, 353, 147, 148, 149 PPC & 7ATA. It is, inter alia, contended that applicants are innocent and have falsely been involved in this case by the police. He contended that as soon as applicants came to know they tried to surrender themselves before the concerned Court for pre-arrest bail but applicants have grave apprehension of their malicious arrest before reaching the same at the hands of police hence, they have approached this Court for grant of protective bail. Be that as it may, without touching merits of the case and in order to enable them to appear before concerned Court they are granted protective bail for a period of Seven days commencing from today, subject to furnishing their solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- each (Rupees Fifty thousand) and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. In the meanwhile operation of NBWs, if any, issued against the applicants is suspended for aforesaid period.

Be that as it may, without touching merits of the case and in order to enable them to appear before concerned Court they are granted protective bail for a period of Seven days commencing from today, subject to furnishing their solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- each (Rupees Fifty thousand) and PR bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. In the meanwhile operation of NBWs, if any, issued against the applicants is suspended for aforesaid period. This order shall cease to have its effect on expiry of said period or on appearance of applicants before concerned Court, whichever is earlier. In case, applicants fail to surrender before the concerned Court within stipulated period, their bail bond shall stand forfeited. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. J U D G E J U D G E Ihsan/PS.
